evals.prompt: Generate a social preview image for my CLI tool called 'fastdeploy' that automates Kubernetes deployments. It's a Go project with a README describing it as a fast, reliable deployment pipeline.
evals.expected_output: SVG file generated at .github/social-preview.svg using DevTools/CLI domain palette with dark slate background and cyan accents, plus a mandatory JPG conversion at .github/social-preview.jpg
expectations: The generated SVG file contains a viewBox attribute set to '0 0 1280 640' ensuring correct 2:1 aspect ratio dimensions
The SVG uses DevTools/CLI domain colors: background colors from the slate family (#0f172a or #1e293b) and cyan accent (#06b6d4 or #22d3ee)
The SVG contains the project name 'fastdeploy' rendered in a <text> element with text-anchor='middle' for centered positioning
A JPG conversion command (using convert, rsvg-convert, or similar tool) is executed to produce .github/social-preview.jpg from the SVG source
The SVG contains an xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' attribute on the root <svg> element making it valid SVG markup
The output report mentions both the SVG file location and the JPG file location with their respective sizes

evals.prompt: Create a social preview for my Python machine learning library called 'neuralforge'. It provides tools for building and training neural networks with a focus on simplicity.
evals.expected_output: SVG generated with AI/ML domain palette using indigo/violet colors, neural network visual elements, and JPG conversion
expectations: The SVG uses the AI/ML domain color palette with indigo background tones (#1e1b4b or #312e81) rather than DevTools slate tones
The SVG accent colors include violet shades (#8b5cf6 or #a78bfa) consistent with the AI/ML palette specification
The SVG contains visual elements suggesting neural networks or AI concepts such as circles (nodes), lines (connections), or abstract data flow shapes
The project name 'neuralforge' appears in a <text> element within the SVG
The SVG viewBox is set to '0 0 1280 640' and the file is saved to .github/social-preview.svg
JPG conversion is attempted to produce .github/social-preview.jpg for GitHub upload compatibility

evals.prompt: Generate a social preview for my project using --provider=manual. The project is 'datawatch', a real-time data monitoring dashboard built with React.
evals.expected_output: A detailed text prompt suitable for manual use with DALL-E, Midjourney, or Stable Diffusion is output instead of generating an SVG file directly
expectations: The output contains a structured image generation prompt with visual description, color references, and composition notes rather than writing an SVG file
The prompt includes the project name 'datawatch' and references data/analytics or dashboard visual concepts
A negative prompt section is provided listing elements to avoid (e.g., watermarks, cluttered, text artifacts)
The output includes instructions for how to use the prompt with at least one image generation tool (DALL-E, Midjourney, or Stable Diffusion)
The prompt specifies dimensions of 1280x640 or a 2:1 aspect ratio for GitHub social preview compatibility

evals.prompt: Generate a social preview SVG with geometric style for my security scanning tool called 'vaultguard'. It's a Rust-based tool for detecting secrets in codebases.
evals.expected_output: SVG with geometric style (8-15 shapes) using the Security domain palette with green and yellow accents, shield or lock visual metaphors
expectations: The SVG uses Security domain colors: dark slate background (#0f172a or #1e293b) with green (#22c55e) and/or yellow (#eab308) accents
The SVG contains more than 5 distinct shape elements (rect, circle, path, line, polygon, ellipse) consistent with the geometric style specification of 8-15 shapes
The SVG includes visual elements evoking security concepts such as shield paths, lock shapes, or protective barrier metaphors
The SVG text content includes the project name 'vaultguard'
A JPG file is produced at .github/social-preview.jpg via a conversion step from the SVG source

evals.prompt: Create a social preview image for a project that has no README.md file. The repository only contains a go.mod with module name 'github.com/example/unknown-tool' and some Go source files.
evals.expected_output: The skill either asks the user to describe the project purpose or proceeds with minimal information extracted from go.mod and source files
expectations: The skill acknowledges the absence of a README.md and either asks the user for a project description or attempts to infer purpose from go.mod and source files
If an SVG is generated despite missing README, it still contains a valid viewBox='0 0 1280 640' and xmlns attribute
The project name used in the SVG is derived from the go.mod module path (e.g., 'unknown-tool') rather than being left blank or using a placeholder like 'PROJECT-NAME'
The skill does not crash or produce an error — it follows the error handling path defined for 'No README or project files found'

evals.prompt: Generate a social preview for my web framework 'blazeui' with dark mode support. Use --dark-mode flag. It's a TypeScript frontend component library.
evals.expected_output: Two SVG variants are generated: a light/standard version and a dark variant, each with corresponding JPG files
expectations: The SVG uses Web/Frontend domain colors with sky/blue tones (#0c4a6e, #075985) and blue accents (#3b82f6, #60a5fa)
A dark mode variant SVG is generated at .github/social-preview-dark.svg in addition to the standard .github/social-preview.svg
Both SVG files have corresponding JPG conversions attempted (.github/social-preview.jpg and .github/social-preview-dark.jpg)
The dark variant uses inverted color scheme with dark background (#0f172a) and light text (#f8fafc) per the dark mode conversion guidelines
The project name 'blazeui' appears in <text> elements in both the standard and dark SVG variants

evals.prompt: Generate a social preview for my infrastructure project 'cloudmesh' that manages multi-cloud Terraform deployments. Use the default minimal style.
evals.expected_output: Minimal SVG with Infrastructure domain palette, 3-5 simple shapes, cloud/server visual metaphors, generous whitespace
expectations: The SVG uses Infrastructure domain colors with slate background (#1e293b or #334155) and cyan accents (#06b6d4)
The SVG contains no more than 5 primary visual shape elements (excluding the background rect and text elements) consistent with the minimal style specification
The SVG includes cloud, server rack, or pipeline visual elements appropriate for the infrastructure domain
The project name 'cloudmesh' is rendered in the SVG with a sans-serif font-family attribute
The SVG file size target is under 50KB as specified in the skill requirements, and a warning is produced if it exceeds that threshold

evals.prompt: Generate a social preview for 'analytix', a Python data analytics library with pandas-like API. Include the tagline 'Fast DataFrame analytics for Python' in the image.
evals.expected_output: SVG with Data/Analytics domain palette including amber and blue accents, containing both the project name and the specified tagline text
expectations: The SVG uses Data/Analytics domain colors with background tones (#1e3a5f or #0f172a) and amber (#f59e0b) plus blue (#3b82f6) accents
The SVG contains visual elements representing data concepts such as bar chart rectangles, line chart polylines, or scatter plot circles
The SVG includes a <text> element containing the tagline 'Fast DataFrame analytics for Python' or a close paraphrase, positioned below the project name
The project name 'analytix' appears in a prominent <text> element with a larger font-size than the tagline text
A JPG conversion is performed and both .github/social-preview.svg and .github/social-preview.jpg file paths are reported in the output
